title: Handcrafted Elytra
description: Adds a craftable, balanced, and highly configurable early game elytra, and dyeable elytras!
body:
# Handcrafted Elytra
Adds a craftable, balanced, and highly configurable early-game elytra, along with support for colored elytras.

## Features
### Core Gameplay
- Adds a **craftable early-game elytra**
- **Crafting recipe**:  
  - 1 String  
  - 1 Leather  
  - 2 Sticks  
  - 4 Phantom Membranes
- Includes a **new advancement** for crafting a handcrafted elytra
- Comes with a **phantom membrane particle trail (Configurable)**
- You can use the jump key to **stop flying mid-flight** with either Elytra **(Configurable)**
- The Handcrafted Elytra is enchantable in the enchanting table. 
- Custom textures and elytra dyeing **(1.21.5+)**

### Durability & Damage (Configurable)
- Base durability is **1/4 of a vanilla elytra**
- Using **firework rockets**:
  - Damages the handcrafted elytra
  - Causes explosive damage to the player 
- Flying **too high** damages the handcrafted elytra
  - There is a sound cue when your elytra is taking more damage.
- Flying **too close to the sun** causes fire damage
- Flying in the **Nether** causes the player fire damage
- Wearing the handcrafted elytra increases **fall damage taken by 50%**
- Uses **armor-style breaking** instead of the vanilla elytra broken state

### Flight Restrictions (Configurable)
- Cannot take off from **water**
- Cannot fly in **rain**

## Elytra Dyeing **(1.21.5+)**
You can dye:
- The **straps** of a handcrafted elytra
- The **wings** of a vanilla elytra

### Dyeing Methods

#### Smithing Table (Preset Colors)
- Use any of the 16 vanilla dyes to produce one of the beautiful, hand-picked preset colors

#### Crafting Table (Vanilla Method)
- Uses vanilla dyeing mechanics to combine dyes to achieve **any color**
- Can be used to reproduce exact hex colors: https://minecraft.wiki/w/Dye#Dyeing_armor

### Important Dyeing Notes
- Only in versions **1.21.5+**
- To remove a dye color, use a **water bottle in a smithing table**
- If a smithing table is used to dye at any point, the color lore becomes permanent
- If you get stuck with a permanent color lore, you can always reset by undyeing the elytra in a smithing table

## Configuration
- All features are **highly customizable!**
- Disable features you don’t like or change values to your preference
- Open the settings menu with: `/function handcrafted_elytra:_settings`

## Known Issues / Limitations
- **Do not repair either elytras in a crafting table!!!** Use an **anvil** or **grindstone** instead. Crafting repairs are intentionally inefficient to prevent infinite vanilla elytra
- Cape compatibility limitations:
  - To use a **cape texture** on your vanilla elytra:
    - Dye your elytra white
    - Enable your cape
  - To use a **colored vanilla elytra**:
    - Disable your cape
  - Mods such as *Capes* let you disable your cape when only wearing an elytra. https://modrinth.com/mod/capes
- The settings menu requires confirmation for each change. To disable command confirmations, use No Command Confirm. https://modrinth.com/mod/nocc
### ⚠ Warning
- For the full intended experience and **textures**, use **1.21.5** or newer.
- Older versions and server-only installs have limited features.

## Installation
There are multiple versions to support your specific needs:

### Data Pack and Resource Pack
- Download both the data pack and resource pack.
- Place the data pack `.zip` file in `world/datapacks`
- Each player must:
  - Add the resource pack `.zip` file as a resource pack
  - Enable resource pack in-game

### Data Resource Pack Combo
- Place the same `.zip` file in `world/datapacks`
- Each player must:
  - Add the same file as a resource pack
  - Enable resource pack in-game

### Mod Wrapper
- Place the `.jar` file in the mods folder on **both the client and the server**

### Server-Side Only
- Datapack: place `.zip` in `world/datapacks`
- Mod wrapper: place the `.jar` file in the server `mods` folder

## Uninstall
To uninstall, use: `/function handcrafted_elytra:uninstall` or the `Uninstall` button in the settings menu.
This will automatically disable the datapack.
